summary:
The claim that none of the listed agents can be used specifically to indicate dentin presence due to their non-dental functions supports selecting option D ("All of the above"). This conclusion is based on evidence showing that each agent lacks specificity and instead has broader biological roles. Thus, collectively they fail to meet the requirement of being reliable indicators for dentin. ```plaintext
Claim: None of the listed agents can reliably indicate dentin presence.
Evidence: Each agent has multiple functions beyond dental tissues; therefore, they are not specific indicators of dentin.
Answer: D
```
